Notice Of Motion And Otion To Compel Answers To Form Interrogatories, Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Dec-10-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
David Edward May, Law Offices of Bruce S. Osterman, 415.399.3900, appearing for the Plaintiff;
No appearance by any other party or counsel.

Ruling: The tentative ruling is adopted as follows: PLAINTIFF JOHN POPPIN'S Motion To Compel Answers To Form Interrogatories, Request For Sanctions is GRANTED-IN-PART. Defendant's supplemental response to Form Interrogatory 15.1 is inadequate as to the Seventh, Ninth, Eleventh, Thirteenth, Nineteenth and Twentieth affirmative defenses. It is not a sufficient response to state that "discovery has just begun and is not yet completed" and reserve the right to supplement at a later date. If Defendant does not have personal knowledge sufficient to respond fully to the interrogatory, he must so state in his response. See CCP 2030.220(c). On the other hand, Defendant's supplemental response to Form Interrogatory 50.5 is adequate because Defendant affirmatively states that he lacks sufficient information in his possession to respond to the interrogatory. See id. With respect to the form interrogatories at issue that ask for the identification of documents, Defendant's response that "[a]ll relevant documents concerning Plaintiff's representation of Infill are in the possession of Plaintiff" is inadequate. Regardless of who possesses the responsive documents, Defendant must identify the responsive documents or affirm that he does not have personal knowledge sufficient to respond to the interrogatory. Defendant's supplemental response to Form Interrogatories 50.1(c), (d) and (f) is adequate because Defendant affirmatively states that he lacks sufficient information in his possession to respond to the interrogatories. Id. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is denied. Given the representations in defense counsel's declaration that (a) settlement talks were happening, (b) he was traveling abroad when plaintiff contacted him on October 11, and (c) he was unable to reach his client until October 25, and further given that Defendant has provided supplemental responses to the interrogatories at issue, the Courts does not find that sanctions are warranted. Anthony Schoenberg, Judge Pro-Tem, Jacqueline Alameda, Clerk, Not Reported. =302/JPT
